description: Wave 1 — Security + Architecture + DocumentRepository God-class + ViewModels

findings.title: allowBackup enabled — sensitive data may leak via Android Backup
findings.problem: The manifest sets `android:allowBackup="true"` globally. This allows backup/restore of sensitive data (auth tokens, AppLock hash, server URL) via adb/recovery. While EncryptedSharedPreferences provides encryption, the Android backup mechanism can extract data during device-compromise scenarios.
findings.proposed_fix: Set `android:allowBackup="false"` in AndroidManifest. If legitimate backup of UI prefs is desired, implement a `BackupAgent` that explicitly excludes sensitive SharedPreferences/DataStore files. See Android Security Guide / Data Storage / Backup.
acceptance_criteria: android:allowBackup is false OR a BackupAgent excludes encrypted/secure storage
No sensitive files appear in `bmgr backupnow` output for this app
Existing tests still pass; `validate-ci.sh` green

findings.title: AppLock lockout constants hardcoded — not configurable, no audit log
findings.problem: Security-critical brute-force constants (MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS=5, LOCKOUT_DURATION_MILLIS=1_800_000, MAX_TOTAL_ATTEMPTS=15) are hardcoded private constants. They cannot be tuned without recompile, and lockout events are never logged for security audits.
findings.proposed_fix: Move constants to TokenManager-backed DataStore prefs with sensible defaults, OR document them explicitly. Emit an INFO-level log (no PII / hash) when a lockout threshold is reached so security audits have a trail.
acceptance_criteria: Constants documented or moved to DataStore with defaults
Lockout-threshold reached emits an audit log without sensitive data
Tests still pass

findings.title: AppLock route reconstruction vulnerable to dual SavedStateHandle desync
findings.problem: `reconstructRouteWithArgs` reads URIs from Navigation BackStackEntry SavedStateHandle, but ViewModel SavedStateHandle is the other half of CLAUDE.md's dual-sync pattern. If they desync, route reconstruction loses data on AppLock unlock OR Process Death recovery fails — depending which handle is stale.
findings.proposed_fix: Introduce a `SavedStateBridge` helper that writes to both handles atomically on every URI/page change. Add debug-build assertion that detects desync at reconstruction time and logs it. Reference CLAUDE.md "AppLock & SavedStateHandle Pattern". Long-term: collapse to a single source of truth (ViewModel SavedStateHandle) and have the interceptor read via a stable accessor.
acceptance_criteria: Atomic dual-write helper used in ScanViewModel + UploadViewModel + DocumentDetailViewModel
Debug assertion catches desync at AppLock unlock
Integration test: lock during scan, unlock — pages preserved
Tests still pass

findings.title: network_security_config permits cleartext HTTP for ALL domains
findings.problem: Base config sets `cleartextTrafficPermitted="true"` globally. The comment mentions self-hosted Paperless without HTTPS, but this allows MITM downgrade for ANY domain. The TokenManager `acceptedHttpHosts` allowlist is only a UI warning — not enforced at the network layer.
findings.proposed_fix: Set base `cleartextTrafficPermitted="false"`. Maintain cleartext only via per-`<domain>` overrides for explicitly accepted hosts. Source the allowlist from DataStore at startup and apply via a `NetworkSecurityConfig.Builder`-like mechanism, OR validate in an OkHttp `Interceptor` before connect. Log HTTP usage at WARN (no URLs).
acceptance_criteria: Base cleartext disabled in network_security_config.xml
Per-host allowlist enforced (manifest overrides or interceptor)
HTTP requests to non-allowlisted hosts fail closed
Tests verify both allow + deny paths

findings.title: Biometric unlock has no rate limit; bypasses PIN brute-force lockout
findings.problem: `unlockWithBiometric()` resets failed-attempt counters on success without rate limiting, and biometric succeeds during a temporary PIN lockout. An attacker who triggers biometric repeatedly (or on a device with biometric-spoof flaws) can bypass the 30-minute PIN lockout entirely.
findings.proposed_fix: (1) Throttle biometric attempts (e.g., one per 5 seconds). (2) On rapid spam, escalate to the same lockout state. (3) Verify BiometricPrompt is configured with `BIOMETRIC_STRONG` and `DEVICE_CREDENTIAL` fallback. (4) Log unlock attempts with timestamp only. Document the trade-off in CLAUDE.md.
acceptance_criteria: Biometric unlock throttled to N per second
Spam triggers temporary lockout shared with PIN path
BiometricPrompt configured with STRONG + credential fallback
Tests verify throttle + escalation

findings.title: TokenManager *Sync() functions runBlocking from OkHttp interceptor — thread starvation risk
findings.problem: `*Sync()` functions wrap async DataStore calls in `runBlocking`. Called from `DynamicBaseUrlInterceptor`, they block OkHttp dispatcher threads. If DataStore IO contends or crypto stalls, the OkHttp thread pool can be starved.
findings.proposed_fix: Cache server URL + token at app startup in an in-process volatile holder with synchronous read. Keep `runBlocking` only in true init/migration paths; never in request interceptors. Expose async `Flow<String?>` for UI. Annotate remaining sync APIs with `@WorkerThread`.
acceptance_criteria: Server URL + token cached at startup, accessed without runBlocking from interceptors
@WorkerThread annotation on remaining sync calls
Stress test: rapid request bursts don't hang the dispatcher
Tests still pass

findings.title: BillingManager state machine missing — null-safety + init race risks
findings.problem: `launchPurchaseFlow()` accesses `billingClient?.launchBillingFlow` without verifying init succeeded. `queryProducts/Purchases` only check `isReady`. If init fails silently the app sits in a half-broken state. Commit cc0b331 fixed crashes via activity-state checks but the underlying state machine is still implicit.
findings.proposed_fix: Introduce explicit `BillingState` enum (UNINITIALIZED/INITIALIZING/READY/FAILED). `initialize()` retries with backoff or transitions to FAILED. All public methods fail fast if not READY and emit an analytics event. Add unit tests for init failure / race scenarios.
acceptance_criteria: BillingState enum + transitions encapsulated in BillingManager
launchPurchaseFlow rejects when not READY (no NPE risk)
Init failure path tested (no leaked listeners, idempotent retry)
Tests still pass

findings.title: AppLock password hash stored without BCrypt format validation
findings.problem: BCrypt hash is stored without sanity checking that the value persists in `$2a$/$2b$/$2y$` format. If DataStore is corrupted or replaced via backup, `BCrypt.checkpw()` silently returns false and the user is locked out without any indication of corruption vs. wrong password.
findings.proposed_fix: Validate BCrypt prefix on write and on read. On format mismatch, log a WARN audit event and force re-setup. Add a migration check at startup. Reference OWASP Password Storage Cheat Sheet.
acceptance_criteria: Format validation present on setup + unlock paths
Hash corruption detected → user re-setup flow
Tests verify good / bad hash behavior

findings.title: AppLockManager init race with TokenManager migration
findings.problem: `shouldLockSync()` calls `tokenManager.hasStoredCredentials()` during init. If TokenManager's plaintext→encrypted migration is still running (or Hilt initialized AppLockManager first), this can return false transiently and AppLock is bypassed even though credentials exist.
findings.proposed_fix: Block until `tokenManager.isMigrationCompleted()` returns true before computing `shouldLockSync()`. Add a debug-build `require()` to fail fast on race. Document the dependency in Hilt module ordering / kdoc.
acceptance_criteria: AppLockManager init waits for TokenManager migration completion
Debug build asserts migration completed when shouldLockSync runs
Integration test: kill app mid-migration, verify state on relaunch

findings.title: No certificate pinning for user-provided Paperless servers (MITM risk)
findings.problem: The app talks to user-provided Paperless servers and trusts system + user CAs. No TLS pinning means a malicious CA / corporate proxy / on-path attacker can intercept tokens and document content over HTTPS.
findings.proposed_fix: Add optional Trust-On-First-Use (TOFU) certificate pinning: capture cert SHA-256 on first successful login (showing fingerprint to user), persist in encrypted storage, and configure OkHttp `CertificatePinner`. Detect cert changes on subsequent connections and surface a warning UI requiring explicit re-trust.
acceptance_criteria: TOFU pinning UI on first login (cert fingerprint displayed)
Pinned hash stored encrypted; OkHttp CertificatePinner enforces it
Cert change triggers a blocking warning dialog
Tests verify pinning + change detection

findings.title: SecureTokenStorage swallows exceptions, masking crypto failures
findings.problem: `getToken()` and `saveToken()` catch all exceptions and return null/false silently. Real failures (`AEADBadTagException`, permission denial, library bug) are indistinguishable from "no token yet". Recovery (`recoverCorruptedStorage`) deletes keystore + prefs non-atomically — partial failure leaves inconsistent state.
findings.proposed_fix: Distinguish exception types: AEAD/corruption → `recoverAndRehydrate()`; PermissionDenied → log security event + propagate; transient → retry with backoff. Make recovery atomic (snapshot + restore on failure). Surface a `Result<T>` to TokenManager so UI can show "please re-login".
acceptance_criteria: Exception taxonomy implemented (recoverable vs hard-fail vs retry)
Recovery is atomic — partial failure is rolled back
Result<T> bubbles to UI for non-recoverable cases
Tests cover each taxonomy branch

findings.title: AppLock state can become inconsistent on logout crash
findings.problem: When MAX_TOTAL_ATTEMPTS is reached, the code clears credentials + AppLock settings in DataStore but only clears `failedAttempts/lockoutUntil` in-memory. A crash between the two leaves inconsistent state: on next launch, no credentials but `LockedOut(permanent=true)` may be reconstructed from stale flags.
findings.proposed_fix: Wrap logout in a single atomic flow that clears DataStore-side state AND in-memory counters in one call. Add an init-time invariant check: if no credentials exist, force Unlocked state and log if a stale LockedOut was found.
acceptance_criteria: Single atomic logout clears all relevant state
Init-time invariant detects + repairs inconsistency
Crash-during-logout test passes

findings.title: BillingManager debug logs reveal product IDs/offer tokens in release
findings.problem: Extensive `Log.d()` calls log product IDs, offer tokens, response codes. While not strictly secret, in release builds these can be captured via Logcat by other apps with READ_LOGS or via ADB on dev devices.
findings.proposed_fix: Gate all `Log.d` behind `BuildConfig.DEBUG` (or use Timber with a release tree that strips DEBUG). Keep ERROR logs always-on but redact tokens. Run `proguard-rules.pro` rule to strip Log.d from release.
acceptance_criteria: Log.d calls compiled out / no-op in release build
ProGuard rule confirms Log.d stripped (verify with apkanalyzer)
ERROR logging continues to redact sensitive payloads

findings.title: Sync TokenManager APIs lack @WorkerThread — IDE can't warn against main-thread misuse
findings.problem: All `*Sync()` blocking helpers lack `@WorkerThread` annotations, so the IDE/lint cannot flag accidental main-thread use, increasing ANR risk for future contributors.
findings.proposed_fix: Add `@WorkerThread` from `androidx.annotation` to every blocking API and document blocking semantics in kdoc.
acceptance_criteria: @WorkerThread annotation on all sync APIs
Lint passes; no main-thread call sites flagged

findings.title: UI ViewModels import PaperlessApi directly — Clean Architecture violation
findings.problem: Diagnostics + Settings ViewModels import `com.paperless.scanner.data.api.PaperlessApi` directly. UI must not depend on Retrofit interfaces — that breaks dependency inversion, prevents swapping implementations, and complicates testing.
findings.proposed_fix: Introduce thin domain repositories (e.g. `ServerStatusRepository`, `DiagnosticsRepository`) that wrap the PaperlessApi calls and return `Result<T>`/`Flow<T>`. Inject those into the ViewModels. Remove all `data.api.*` imports from `ui/`.
acceptance_criteria: No data.api.* imports under ui/
Each removed call is wrapped in a repository method
Repository methods return Result<T> or Flow<T>
Tests still pass

findings.title: UploadViewModel constructor explosion (16 dependencies) — SRP violation
findings.problem: UploadViewModel takes 16 constructor dependencies (Context, SavedStateHandle, 4 entity repositories, queue + worker manager, network + server-health monitors, analytics, suggestion orchestrator, AI usage repo, premium feature manager, token manager, dispatcher). This is a clear God-VM signal.
findings.proposed_fix: Extract a `UploadCoordinator`/`PerformUploadUseCase` that owns orchestration (queue + workers + analytics + monitors). Reduce VM to 4–5 dependencies (coordinator, suggestion source, premium gate, dispatchers). Move dispatcher injection behind a `CoroutineDispatchers` bundle.
acceptance_criteria: UploadViewModel constructor ≤ 5 params
Upload orchestration moved to UseCase/Coordinator class with own tests
All UploadScreen flows still pass UI tests

findings.title: Repositories return suspend Result<T> for observable data — should be Flow
findings.problem: List/detail getters use `suspend fun ... Result<T>` requiring callers to manually re-fetch on changes. CLAUDE.md mandates reactive Flow / Room Flow for observable data.
findings.proposed_fix: Convert read paths to `fun observeX(): Flow<...>` backed by Room Flow. Reserve `suspend` for pure-side-effect operations (login, upload). Use `stateIn(scope, SharingStarted.WhileSubscribed(5_000), initial)` to share state across collectors.
acceptance_criteria: Document list + detail expose Flow
Caching uses Room Flow not manual suspend fetch+update
ViewModels removed manual refresh hacks
Tests verify reactive update on DB write

findings.title: AppModule uses @Provides for all bindings — no @Binds for interfaces
findings.problem: All Hilt bindings use `@Provides` returning concrete types (Retrofit `PaperlessApi`, Gson, OkHttpClient). No interface seams → tests must rebuild full HTTP stack to inject fakes.
findings.proposed_fix: Extract `PaperlessApiClient` interface (or a domain-shaped façade). Use `@Binds abstract fun bindApi(impl: PaperlessApiImpl): PaperlessApiClient`. Test module overrides binding with fake. Same for OkHttp configuration where helpful.
acceptance_criteria: PaperlessApi hidden behind interface, repositories depend on interface
Test module successfully swaps in a fake implementation
No direct concrete-class injection in production repositories

findings.title: Navigation routes use untyped string concatenation — typo-prone
findings.problem: Routes like `"scan?pageUris={pageUris}"` are strings; missing URL encoding silently fails, typos compile. Hardcoded literal strings appear at navigate() call sites.
findings.proposed_fix: Adopt typed Navigation 2.8+ with `@Serializable` route data classes, OR centralize route construction via factory functions on each `Screen` and forbid raw string literals at call sites (lint rule).
acceptance_criteria: All navigate() calls use typed/factory routes — no raw strings in screens
URL encode/decode centralized + tested
Migration plan documented for any deferred routes

findings.title: Upload endpoint plain-string parsing fragile — no defensive helper / test
findings.problem: Paperless-ngx returns the upload task UUID as a plain string (with optional surrounding quotes). Current parsing relies on `response.string().trim().removeSurrounding("\"")` inline at call sites. No dedicated parser, no test pinning the format.
findings.proposed_fix: Add `fun parseUploadTaskId(body: ResponseBody): Result<String>` with explicit nullability + format checks. Add unit test pinned against a captured raw response. Reference CLAUDE.md API quirks section.
acceptance_criteria: Dedicated parser used at all upload call sites
Test fixture for the known plain-string response format
Malformed body returns Failure with diagnostic message

findings.title: ViewModel resolves user-facing strings via injected Context — UI concern leaked into VM
findings.problem: ViewModels inject `@ApplicationContext` and call `context.getString(R.string.X)` to produce error strings. CLAUDE.md mandates: error codes from data layer, string resolution in UI layer (`stringResource`).
findings.proposed_fix: Replace VM error states with sealed `UploadError` types carrying string-resource IDs. Composables resolve via `stringResource(error.messageRes)`. Reserve VM-side Context only for non-localized work (file URIs, settings).
acceptance_criteria: No context.getString in VM error paths
UploadError sealed type carries @StringRes ids
Compose layer resolves all user-facing strings

findings.title: AuthRepository builds raw OkHttp requests — bypasses Retrofit layer
findings.problem: AuthRepository injects OkHttpClient directly to do dynamic protocol detection (raw `okhttp3.Request`). This creates a parallel HTTP layer with its own timeout/retry, inconsistent with the Retrofit-based API surface.
findings.proposed_fix: Extract a `ProtocolDetector` service that owns the raw HTTP probe with explicit short timeout. AuthRepository becomes a façade: detection → server URL set → Retrofit calls. Centralize timeout configuration.
acceptance_criteria: ProtocolDetector class with single responsibility
AuthRepository no longer references OkHttpClient directly
Probe timeouts documented + tested

findings.title: Dual SavedStateHandle sync pattern lacks compile-time safety
findings.lines: manual sync sites in ScanScreen, BatchImportScreen, MultiPageUploadScreen
findings.problem: CLAUDE.md documents two SavedStateHandles (ViewModel + Navigation BackStackEntry) that must be kept manually in sync via `LaunchedEffect`. Any forgotten sync silently breaks AppLock OR Process Death recovery.
findings.proposed_fix: Centralize behind a `rememberDualSavedState(viewModel, backStackEntry, key)` helper that asserts both writes happen atomically. Long-term consider a single source of truth + a stable accessor used by AppLockNavigationInterceptor.
acceptance_criteria: rememberDualSavedState helper + tests
All screens migrated to helper, no raw LaunchedEffect copies
Process death + AppLock paths tested per screen

findings.title: Repositories lack stateIn / shareIn — re-collection re-queries DB
findings.problem: Suspend `getAll()` getters re-execute on every call. Even where a Flow exists, no `stateIn`/`shareIn` means each collector pays the DB round-trip on recomposition.
findings.proposed_fix: Convert to `fun observeAll(): StateFlow<List<T>>` shared with `stateIn(scope, SharingStarted.WhileSubscribed(5_000), emptyList())`. Document the sharing strategy.
acceptance_criteria: Hot-cached entity flows use stateIn or shareIn
Recomposition no longer triggers redundant DB queries
Tests verify cached emission to late subscribers

findings.title: DocumentRepository is a 1349-line God-class with 40+ mixed responsibilities
findings.problem: Single class spans PDF creation, image compression, document CRUD, paging, search, trash, permissions, notes, history, user/group management, tag operations and pending-change tracking. SRP and testability are wrecked.
findings.proposed_fix: Decompose per the Decomposition Plan in `docs/code-reviews/REFACTOR_DOCUMENT_REPOSITORY.md`: 8 specialized repositories + 3 service classes (ImageProcessor, PdfGenerator, DocumentSerializer). Use 5-phase migration with façade for backward compat.
acceptance_criteria: DocumentRepository becomes a delegating façade
Each new repo ≤ 250 LOC, single responsibility
Existing callers compile unchanged via façade
Each new repo has dedicated tests
Phased migration documented + executed

findings.title: Upload pipeline couples PDF creation, compression, IO, API in single 80-line method
findings.problem: Upload functions mix multipart form construction, iText PDF creation, bitmap sampling/compression, file IO, error mapping. Each block could change independently but is fused.
findings.proposed_fix: Extract `ImageProcessorService` (sampling + compression + URI→bytes), `PdfGeneratorService` (iText), and a thin `DocumentUploadRepository` that orchestrates them. Each service ≤ 100 LOC.
acceptance_criteria: ImageProcessorService + PdfGeneratorService extracted with own tests
DocumentUploadRepository orchestrates and maps results to Result<T>
Existing upload flow tests still green

findings.title: Query/paging/filter logic inlined and duplicated across 5 functions
findings.problem: Five list/count functions duplicate paging math + filter joining + cache fallback inline. `DocumentFilterQueryBuilder` is invoked per-call with manual tag-string building (L523).
findings.proposed_fix: Create `DocumentListRepository` with unified `observeDocuments(filter, page, pageSize): Flow<PagingData<Document>>`. Move tag filtering into a `TagFilterStrategy`. Push raw query building into the DAO layer.
acceptance_criteria: Five functions consolidated behind a DocumentListRepository façade
No raw tag-string concatenation in repository
Paging size parameter is documented + injectable

findings.title: Trash/restore/permanent-delete duplicated across single + bulk variants
findings.target: DocumentRepository.observeTrashDocuments, observeTrashCount, getTrashDocuments, restoreDocument, restoreDocuments, permanentlyDeleteDocument, permanentlyDeleteDocuments
findings.problem: Single + bulk variants for restore (57 + 60 LOC) and permanent delete (58 + 60 LOC) duplicate state-clearing and sync logic. Trash domain doesn't belong in DocumentRepository.
findings.proposed_fix: Extract `TrashRepository` exposing observe + restore (single+bulk) + permanently-delete (single+bulk) + retention helpers. Deduplicate state mutation via private `ensureDeletionStateCleared()` and `syncDeletedIdsWithServer()`.
acceptance_criteria: TrashRepository encapsulates all trash flows
No duplicate sync/state-clearing logic between single + bulk paths
Retention window parameterized + tested

findings.title: Repository resolves user-facing error strings via Context.getString
findings.problem: Repository calls `context.getString(R.string.error_*)` directly. This couples data layer to Android resources, violates Clean Architecture, and breaks unit testability without Robolectric.
findings.proposed_fix: Refactor `PaperlessException` to carry typed error codes only. Move string resolution to ViewModel/UI. Inject `@StringRes` IDs via the exception or sealed error type.
acceptance_criteria: No context.getString call in DocumentRepository
UI layer maps exceptions to localized strings
Repository unit tests no longer require Context

findings.title: Permissions/users/groups (~75 LOC) belong in their own access-control repository
findings.problem: User/group/permission methods are bundled into DocumentRepository for convenience but represent a different domain — access control. They are pure API pass-throughs without document logic.
findings.proposed_fix: Extract `PermissionRepository` with these three methods. Document mutations import it for permission resolution; dependency direction Document→Permission only.
acceptance_criteria: PermissionRepository owns user/group/permission methods
DocumentMetadataRepository depends on PermissionRepository, not vice versa
Tests for both repositories independent

findings.title: Audit/notes endpoints are thin API wrappers — extract to AuditRepository
findings.problem: History + note functions have no caching/sync logic — just API call + map. Logically belong with audit/annotation operations, not document CRUD.
findings.proposed_fix: Move into `AuditRepository`. Keep contract Result<T> + suspend (no offline behavior since audit is server-only).
acceptance_criteria: AuditRepository contains history + notes
DocumentRepository façade delegates
Tests for AuditRepository independent

findings.title: Online/offline branching mixed into CRUD methods — sync coupling
findings.problem: `updateDocument()` checks `serverHealthMonitor.isServerReachable.value`, branches into either API call or pending-change insertion with manual JSON. Same pattern repeats for delete/restore. CRUD intent mixed with sync state machine.
findings.proposed_fix: Extract `DocumentSyncRepository.executeOrQueue(operation, entityId, payload)`. CRUD methods delegate to it. Centralize PendingChange serialization (use Gson, see F-033) + queue management.
acceptance_criteria: CRUD methods stop touching ServerHealthMonitor directly
DocumentSyncRepository owns branching + queue
Offline mutation returns optimistic cached result
Sync-on-reconnect flushes queue

findings.title: Pending-change JSON built via manual buildString — fragile, untyped
findings.problem: Manual JSON construction with `buildString { append("{...}") }` and trailing-comma trimming is error-prone. No type safety; sync-time deserialization can silently drop fields.
findings.proposed_fix: Define typed `PendingChangeRequest` data class mirroring `UpdateDocumentRequest`. Use injected Gson for serialize/deserialize. Reuse model types between live request + queued change.
acceptance_criteria: Manual JSON construction removed
PendingChangeRequest typed and shared with API request type
Round-trip test asserts no field loss

findings.title: Crashlytics breadcrumbs scattered across upload paths with hardcoded strings
findings.problem: Breadcrumb labels ("UPLOAD_START", "UPLOAD_SUCCESS", "UPLOAD_ERROR") are hardcoded inline at multiple call sites. Refactoring upload code drifts breadcrumbs out of sync.
findings.proposed_fix: Introduce `UploadMetricsTracker` with named methods (`logStart(pageCount)`, `logSuccess(taskId)`, `logError(errorType)`). Inject Crashlytics helper. Single source of truth for breadcrumb labels.
acceptance_criteria: UploadMetricsTracker centralizes breadcrumb logging
No hardcoded breadcrumb strings in DocumentRepository
Tests verify tracker calls via mock

findings.title: Custom-field + tag JSON serialization scattered with TypeToken duplication
findings.problem: Gson serialization for custom fields and tag lists is duplicated in upload paths. `TypeToken<List<Int>>()` appears once but the pattern is fragile and reused inconsistently.
findings.proposed_fix: Create `DocumentSerializer` with `serializeCustomFields/serializeTagList/deserializeTagList` etc. Centralize Gson dependency.
acceptance_criteria: DocumentSerializer encapsulates custom field + tag serialization
No TypeToken instantiation in repository code
Reused across upload, update, sync paths

findings.title: Five count functions with divergent strategies — no unified count API
findings.problem: Five count functions each take different inputs, sometimes call DAO, sometimes raw query builder, sometimes accept `forceRefresh`. Caching strategy differs per function.
findings.proposed_fix: Create `DocumentCountRepository` exposing a single `observeCount(filter, searchQuery, forceRefresh): Flow<Int>` with thin wrappers for trash/untagged. Push query construction down to DAO.
acceptance_criteria: DocumentCountRepository owns all counts
Single primary method + thin wrappers
Cache strategy uniform across counts

findings.title: Suspend scope ownership unclear — long upload jobs cancel with VM
findings.lines: L73, L153, L441, L488 (and many more)
findings.problem: Suspend functions inherit caller scope. Upload from `viewModelScope` cancels when the user navigates away. Long-running operations (multi-page upload, batch restore) silently abort with no resume strategy.
findings.proposed_fix: Document scope ownership per public method (kdoc). For long-running mutations move to a WorkManager job or `applicationScope`. Persist intent before suspending so reconnect can resume.
acceptance_criteria: Scope ownership documented per method
Long uploads survive screen navigation (Worker or applicationScope)
Pending changes flushed on reconnect even after VM cancellation
Tests verify cancellation behavior

findings.title: No retry/backoff on API calls — transient errors fail immediately
findings.problem: API calls use plain try/catch with no exponential backoff or retry policy. Transient 5xx + timeout errors fail immediately. Large multi-page uploads have no resume support.
findings.proposed_fix: Introduce `RetryPolicy` (max 3 retries, exponential backoff with jitter, retry only idempotent ops + 429/5xx). Wrap API calls via a helper `apiWithRetry { ... }`. For uploads consider partial-PDF resume.
acceptance_criteria: RetryPolicy used for idempotent operations
Non-idempotent ops not retried (avoid duplicate uploads)
Tests simulate transient failures + verify retry count

findings.title: Tag-count sync not transactional — stale tag counts on partial failure
findings.problem: `updateTagDocumentCounts()` mutates tag counts without a Room transaction wrapping the document update. Partial DAO failure leaves counts diverged. `getOldTagIds()` returns `emptyList()` on JSON parse failure, silently losing history.
findings.proposed_fix: Wrap document + tag-count mutations in a single `@Transaction` Room method. Log + propagate JSON parse failures rather than swallowing them. Optionally extract `TagSyncService` for clarity.
acceptance_criteria: Document + tag-count update happens in single Room transaction
JSON parse failures logged + surfaced (no silent emptyList)
Test verifies atomic rollback on tag DAO failure

findings.title: Class- and method-level kdoc absent for cache/offline-first semantics
findings.problem: Kdoc references "offline-first" and "BEST PRACTICE" but never explains cache TTL, invalidation triggers, soft-delete inclusion semantics, or pending-change interaction.
findings.proposed_fix: Add class-level kdoc covering: cache hierarchy, TTL/refresh policy, soft-delete query rules, pending-change behavior. Per public method: when does it refresh from server? Is cached data filtered for soft deletes?
acceptance_criteria: Class kdoc explains cache hierarchy + TTL
Each Flow function documents refresh + soft-delete rules
Pending change behavior documented at method level

findings.title: Decomposition plan reference (refactor roadmap)
findings.problem: DocumentRepository must be split into 8 specialized repositories + 3 services. Without a phased plan, decomposition risks introducing regressions and breaking façade contracts.
findings.proposed_fix: Follow the 5-phase migration documented in `docs/code-reviews/REFACTOR_DOCUMENT_REPOSITORY.md` (services → reactive repos → specialized repos → permission repo → façade + caller migration). Total ~13 dev-days; sprintable.
acceptance_criteria: Phase 1 (services) merged + green
Phase 2 (reactive repos) merged + green
Phase 3 (specialized repos) merged + green
Phase 4 (permission repo) merged + green
Phase 5 (façade + caller migration) merged + green

findings.title: HomeViewModel.tagMap is mutable shared state without thread safety
findings.problem: `tagMap = tags.associateBy { it.id }` writes to a shared mutable var read by multiple reactive flows. No synchronization — concurrent collectors can read partial maps, causing null tag refs.
findings.proposed_fix: Convert to `private val _tagMap = MutableStateFlow<Map<Int, Tag>>(emptyMap())`. Replace direct assignment with `.update {}`. Consumers collect from the StateFlow; combine where needed.
acceptance_criteria: tagMap is a private StateFlow
All updates use .update { } block syntax
Consumers collect via Flow not direct field access
Test verifies concurrent updates do not lose entries

findings.title: HomeViewModel: 17+ viewModelScope.launch with inconsistent error handling
findings.problem: ~15 separate launch{collect{}} blocks each handle errors differently — log/swallow/null-update. One repository throwing = silent failure or inconsistent UI state.
findings.proposed_fix: Wrap repo flows with a shared helper that maps to `Result<T>` (e.g. `Flow<T>.asUiResult()`). Centralize error UI state into a single `_errorState: StateFlow<HomeError?>`. Standardize onError handling.
acceptance_criteria: All observe* flows funnel into a shared Result<T> helper
Single error StateFlow owns user-visible errors
Tests verify error propagation + recovery

findings.title: ScanViewModel uses imperative .toMutableList() outside StateFlow.update
findings.problem: Page mutations create intermediate `MutableList` outside the StateFlow update block, then sync to SavedStateHandle separately. Forgetting the sync silently loses pages on Process Death/AppLock.
findings.proposed_fix: Move all mutations inside `_uiState.update { state -> state.copy(pages = ...) }`. Consolidate SavedStateHandle sync into a helper invoked from a single update path.
acceptance_criteria: No .toMutableList outside .update {}
Single helper updates state + syncs SavedStateHandle atomically
Tests verify atomic mutation

findings.title: ScanViewModel does not sync pages to Navigation BackStackEntry SavedStateHandle
findings.problem: Pages are written only to ViewModel SavedStateHandle. Per CLAUDE.md AppLock & SavedStateHandle Pattern, the Navigation BackStackEntry SavedStateHandle must also be updated, otherwise AppLock route reconstruction loses pages.
findings.proposed_fix: Centralize via `updatePagesAndSync(pages)` that updates `_uiState`, ViewModel SavedStateHandle, AND Navigation BackStackEntry SavedStateHandle (passed in via screen LaunchedEffect). Provide a `rememberDualSavedState` Compose helper.
acceptance_criteria: Both SavedStateHandles updated on every page mutation
AppLock unlock test: scan, lock, unlock — pages restored
Process Death test: same pages survive
Tests pass

findings.title: UploadViewModel race: setDocumentUris vs reactive documentUrisStateFlow
findings.problem: Screen calls `setDocumentUris()` after VM creation, but reactive `documentUrisStateFlow` reads from SavedStateHandle. Order of operations means flow may emit empty list before manual set, racing dependent flows.
findings.proposed_fix: Pass URIs into VM via assisted-injection / Hilt @AssistedFactory or read from BackStackEntry args inside the VM at init. Remove `setDocumentUris`. Alternatively expose a single source of truth `MutableStateFlow` written atomically with the SavedStateHandle.
acceptance_criteria: URIs set during VM construction (no post-init mutator)
No race between manual set and reactive read
Tests verify URI availability before any dependent flow emits

findings.title: UploadViewModel does not sync document URIs to Navigation BackStackEntry SavedStateHandle
findings.problem: Same pattern as F-045: VM writes to ViewModel SavedStateHandle only; Navigation BackStackEntry is untouched. AppLock unlock loses URIs.
findings.proposed_fix: Use the `rememberDualSavedState` helper (F-023). Sync both handles on every URI change.
acceptance_criteria: Dual SavedStateHandle updated on every URI mutation
AppLock + Process Death tests cover URI persistence
Tests pass

findings.title: HomeViewModel is a 1328-line God-VM with 11+ reactive flows
findings.problem: Single VM owns: stats, recent docs, processing tasks, untagged + deleted counts, trash retention, pending changes, active uploads, failed syncs, tag creation, suggestions, AI, network/health monitors. 1328 LOC; mocking requires 15+ deps.
findings.proposed_fix: Split into composable sub-VMs (DocumentStatsViewModel, ProcessingTasksViewModel, TagManagementViewModel, ServerHealthViewModel) and have HomeViewModel become a thin coordinator. Or use `composable<Route>` + scoped sub-VMs per section.
acceptance_criteria: HomeViewModel ≤ 600 LOC
Each sub-VM ≤ 300 LOC and unit-testable in isolation
All HomeScreen sections still functional

findings.title: DocumentDetailViewModel uses local mutable lists in async coroutines
findings.problem: Two `mutableListOf()` populated asynchronously, then assigned to state. Concurrent calls can interleave + duplicate entries. No thread safety.
findings.proposed_fix: Combine fetches in a single suspend function returning `Pair<List<UserInfo>, List<GroupInfo>>` and update state atomically with `_uiState.update { it.copy(...) }`.
acceptance_criteria: No mutableListOf created inside coroutines for shared state
Single suspend function returns immutable result
Atomic state update

findings.title: LabelsViewModel exposes mutable vars (allTags, allCorrespondents, …) with race risk
findings.problem: Four `private var` lists are updated inside reactive flows but read by other methods (`getActiveEntities`, `applyCurrentFilters`). Read-during-write races; late subscribers don't replay last value.
findings.proposed_fix: Convert each to `MutableStateFlow<List<EntityItem>>`. All mutations go through `.update {}`. Expose a derived `displayedEntities: StateFlow<List<EntityItem>>` that filters/applies search reactively.
acceptance_criteria: Four mutable vars replaced with StateFlow
displayedEntities is derived via combine() with no manual triggers
Tests verify reactive filter updates

findings.title: HomeViewModelTest covers <20% of reactive flows
findings.problem: Existing test file is small (~200 LOC) compared to a 1328-LOC VM. 15+ reactive flows lack coverage for error paths, SavedStateHandle sync, tagMap races, and flow cancellation.
findings.proposed_fix: Add `@OptIn(ExperimentalCoroutinesApi)` tests with `UnconfinedTestDispatcher`. Use `Turbine` for Flow assertions. Replace mocks with fakes per CLAUDE.md "don't mock the database" guidance.
acceptance_criteria: ≥ 90% coverage on observe* methods
Tests cover error + success + cancellation paths
Fakes used in place of repository mocks

findings.title: ScanViewModel has no tests for SavedStateHandle sync
findings.problem: ScanViewModel performs critical SavedStateHandle sync but no unit tests verify pages survive process death, sync writes happen on every mutation, or sync failures don't corrupt state.
findings.proposed_fix: Create `ScanViewModelTest` with fixtures using `SavedStateHandle()` directly. Verify: pages restored from initial state, syncPagesToSavedState is called on every mutation, page move + remove preserve metadata.
acceptance_criteria: ≥ 80% coverage on page mutation + sync
Tests verify SavedStateHandle reflects ViewModel state
Refactors of L4 (F-045) gated on these tests

findings.title: UploadViewModel test does not cover documentUris dual-state sync
findings.problem: Existing UploadViewModelTest (686 LOC) does not assert: URIs restored after process death, setDocumentUris() syncs to SavedStateHandle, race between manual set + reactive read.
findings.proposed_fix: Add fixtures: `documentUrisRestoredFromSavedState`, `setDocumentUris_writesToSavedStateHandle`, `documentUris_raceCondition_holds`.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover setDocumentUris → SavedStateHandle path
Process Death restoration test passes
Race condition test passes

findings.title: DocumentDetailViewModel does not sync documentId to Navigation BackStackEntry
findings.problem: Reactive `documentIdStateFlow` reads from ViewModel SavedStateHandle. Navigation BackStackEntry SavedStateHandle is never updated → AppLock unlock loses the documentId.
findings.proposed_fix: Use the dual-sync helper from F-023. Update both handles whenever documentId changes.
acceptance_criteria: Both handles updated on documentId change
AppLock unlock test passes
Process Death test passes

findings.title: HomeViewModel: tagMap-dependent flow can race with tag-load flow → null tag refs
findings.problem: If `observeRecentDocumentsReactively` emits before `observeTagsReactively` populates tagMap, `firstTagId?.let { tagMap[it] }` returns null intermittently. Order depends on launch timing.
findings.proposed_fix: Use `combine(tagRepository.observeTags(), documentRepository.observeRecentDocuments(...)) { tags, docs -> ... }` so both sources are guaranteed before mapping.
acceptance_criteria: Tag-dependent flows use combine() instead of separate observers + map
Tests verify no null tag references under varied launch order

findings.title: ScanViewModel + UploadViewModel: repeated withContext context switches inside one coroutine
findings.lines: L494, L504, L605, L621 (Scan); UploadViewModel L241, L334, L409, L524, L658, L704
findings.target: ScanViewModel.cropPage and similar; UploadViewModel.* upload methods
findings.problem: Multiple `withContext(Dispatchers.IO)` ↔ `Dispatchers.Main` switches inside the same coroutine cost dispatcher hops without benefit.
findings.proposed_fix: Establish IO context once at function entry; switch to Main only at the final state-update step. Or push state updates through `MutableStateFlow` (thread-safe, no context switch needed).
acceptance_criteria: ≤ 2 context switches per coroutine
IO boundary established at function start
Profiler confirms reduction in dispatcher hops

findings.title: HomeViewModel polls server health instead of observing reactive monitor
findings.problem: Polls in a `while(true) { delay; healthCheck() }` loop despite `serverHealthMonitor.isServerReachable` already being a reactive StateFlow.
findings.proposed_fix: Drop the polling job. Collect `serverHealthMonitor.isServerReachable` directly. If polling is needed, do it inside the monitor — not in every consumer.
acceptance_criteria: No polling loop in HomeViewModel
Server health observed via reactive Flow
Tests verify reactive update without polling

findings.title: All ViewModels: viewModelScope coroutines lack timeouts — UI hangs on slow repos
findings.target: All five ViewModels (~80 launch{} call sites)
findings.problem: Repository flows have no timeout. If the Flow hangs (network, deadlock), the coroutine sits forever, UI never recovers.
findings.proposed_fix: Wrap loading paths in `withTimeoutOrNull(REASONABLE_TIMEOUT)` or use `Flow<T>.timeout(...)`. On timeout emit a typed error so UI can show a retry CTA.
acceptance_criteria: Loading flows have timeouts (config per use case)
Timeout produces a user-visible error + retry option
Tests verify timeout path

findings.title: LabelsViewModel.init triggers refresh() synchronously without error capture
findings.problem: `init { refresh() }` runs at construction. Any exception thrown propagates from constructor; observers never see an error state.
findings.proposed_fix: Move refresh into an observer-first path: have refreshing be a side effect of starting reactive flows, OR `init { viewModelScope.launch { refresh() } }` wrapped in try/catch that updates `_uiState` with a failure.
acceptance_criteria: VM construction no longer throws on refresh error
Failure surfaces as UI error state
Tests verify error path
